events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "DP impulse lines incorrectly sloped for service: gas service taps located below the process pipe, liquid/steam service taps located on top of the process pipe."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on this transmitter installation and issue a non-conformance report.",
    "Re-route impulse lines per manufacturer's installation manual and API RP 551: for gas service, taps must be on top or side with continuous slope up to transmitter; for liquid/steam, taps must be on side with continuous slope down.",
    "Re-inspect after correction before closing out the rough-in stage."
  ],
  "rationale": "Incorrect impulse line slope causes condensate accumulation in gas legs (blockage/damping) or gas pockets in liquid legs (loss of head/measurement error). This is a fundamental installation defect that renders the transmitter unreliable and is explicitly prohibited by API RP 551 and manufacturer guidelines. Must be failed and corrected before service.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 (Process Measurement Instrumentation)",
    "Transmitter manufacturer installation manual"
  ]
}
